original: A flexible battery capable of being charged wirelessly and folded and stretched is developed
1st flight of Space Shuttle (atop a Boeing 747)
Global Seed Vault in Svalbard, Norway, receives its 1 millionth seed on its 10 year anniversary
Scientists publish findings into "lost" continent Balkanatolia, that linked southern Europe with Asia, providing passageway for animal migrations 35 to 38 million years ago
2 Clydesdale horses set record by pulling 48 tons on a sledge, in Michigan
RADAR (Radio Detection and Ranging) is first demonstrated byRobert Watson-Wattand Arnold Wilkins in Daventry, England
Michael Jackson's "Thriller" album goes #1 and stays #1 for 37 weeks
NBA'sMichael Jordan's 58 points in one game is a Chicago Bulls record
Tex Avery, American cartoonist at Warner Bros, MGM (Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, Porky Pig), born in Taylor, Texas (d. 1980)
William "Buffalo Bill" Cody, Wild West hunter and showman (Buffalo Bill's Wild West), born in Davenport Iowa (d. 1917)
John Harvey Kellogg, American surgeon and nutritionist, inspired flaked cereal industry, born in Tyrone, Michigan (d. 1943)
Last total eclipse of Sun in 20th century for continental US
Cold weather nicknamed "the Beast from the East" hits Europe, killing 7 and covering Pompeii in snow
Winter storm deposits snow in parts of southern California for the first time in decades, down to 1,000 feet elevation, with snow accumulations up to 7 feet deep in San Bernardino Mountains
Margo Hayesbecomes the first woman to climb a 9a+ (5.15a) graded route by ascending La Rambla in Siurana, Spain
US Congress establishes Grand Canyon National Park in Arizona
Global Seed Vault officially opens in Svalbard, Norway, to help preserve diversity and loss from other genebanks
Pauline Musters, Dutch world's smallest woman at 24 inches (61 cm), born in Ossendrecht, Netherlands (d. 1895)
Nicolas-Joseph Cugnot, French inventor who designed and built 1st automobile, born in Void, France (d. 1804) [or born September 25]
USA's David Jenkins wins Olympic Gold for men's figure skating
The Roman Inquisition delivers injunction toGalileodemanding he abandon his belief in heliocentrism, which states the Earth and planets revolve around the Sun
French Train Grande Vitesse averages 380 kph on trial run
Total solar eclipse in Venezuela-Pacific Ocean, lasts for 4 minutes and 9 seconds
UK reaches its highest-ever winter temperature with 21.2°C (70.2F) in Kew Gardens, London
'Rain bomb' due to a slow-moving low pressure trough begins major flooding in south-east Queensland, Australia, leaving at least seven people dead
1st red & green traffic lights installed in Manhattan, NYC
Golden Gate Bridge groundbreaking ceremony held at Crissy Field
First known European landing in Australia by Dutch navigator Willem Janszoon at the Pennefather River, Cape York, northern Australia
Michael Owens of Toledo, Ohio, patents a glass-blowing machine to make glass bottles
1st buffalo purchased for Golden Gate Park
Napoléon Bonaparte and his supporters leave Elba to start a 100-day reconquest of France
US PresidentCalvin Coolidgeestablishes Grand Teton National Park
HMHS Britannic, sister to the Titanic, is launched at Harland & Wolff, Belfast
Toymaker Hasbro announces that the Mr Potato Head brand name will be changed to be gender-neutral
First death from measles in the US occurs in Texas in a decade, and the first child to die in 22 years amid an outbreak affecting 124 people
Camille Flammarion, French astronomer, Mars researcher and popularizer of astronomy, born in Montigny-le-Roi, Haute-Marne, France (d. 1925)
Ahmed Zewail, Egyptian-American chemist and Nobel laureate (1999), born in Damanhur, Egypt (d. 2016)
Keena Rothhammer, American swimmer (Olympic gold 800m freestyle 1972 WR 8:53.68; World C'ship gold 200m freestyle 1973), born in Little Rock, Arkansas
Rie Van Veen swims world record 200m free style (2:24.6)
Rie Mastenbroek, Dutch swimmer (3 x Olympic gold 100m freestyle, 4x100m freestyle relay, 400m freestyle 1936; silver 100m backstroke 1936), born in Rotterdam, Netherlands (d. 2003)
Wash blocks 20 Indiana shots tying NBA regulation game record
84 penalties (406 mins) assessed for a brawl between NHL Minn & Bost
Allan Borderbeats Gavaskar's record for most Test Cricket runs 10,123
West Indies make 1st Test Cricket win, by 289 runs over England
ICC Women's Cricket T20 World Cup, Cape Town: Defending champions Australia score a 19-run victory over South Africa to win their sixth title; Player of the series: Australian all-rounder Ash Gardner

Grover Cleveland Alexander, American baseball HOF pitcher (World Series 1926 Chicago Cubs; Triple Crown 1915, 16, 20; 6 × NL wins & strikeout leader; Philadelphia Phillies), born in Elba, Nebraska (d. 1950)
Susan J. Helms, Mjr USAF/Astronaut (STS 54, 64, 78), born in Charlotte, North Carolina
J. T. Snow, American baseball first baseman (6 x Gold Glove; San Francisco Giants), born in Long Beach, California
Joe Mullen, American Hockey Hall of Fame right wing (Stanley Cup 1989, Calgary Flames; Pittsburgh Penguins 1991, 92; NHL All Star 1988, 89), born in New York City
Rolando Blackman, American basketball guard (NBA All-Star 1985, 86, 87, 90; Dallas Mavs, NY Knicks; Italian Cup MVP 1996 Olimpia Milano), born in Panama City, Panama
Jonathan Cain, American rock guitarist & keyboardist (Journey; Babys), born in Chicago, Illinois
Keisuke Kuwata, Japanese pop singer,songwriter and film score composer, born in Chigasaki, Japan
Yōsuke Yamashita, Japanese jazz pianist, and composer of jazz, film scores, and classical works, born in Tokyo, Japan
Anatoli Vassilyevich Filipchenko, Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz 7; Soyuz, 16), born in Davydovka, USSR (d. 2022)
Arsene Souffriau, Belgian pioneering electronic music composer, born in Ixelles, Belgium (d. 2012)
Theodore Sturgeon, American sci-fi author (Hugo, It, Caviar), born in Staten Island, New York (d. 1985)
Chauncey Haughton, American jazz clarinetist, and saxophonist (Blanche Calloway; Chick Webb; Cab Calloway), born in Chestertown, Maryland (d. 1989)
William Russell [Russell William Wagner], American violinist, modern composer (Fugue for Eight Percussion Instruments), and musicologist (Oh, Mister Jelly), born in Canton, Missouri (d. 1992)
Ida Cox (nee Prather), American blues singer and vaudeville performer, born in Habersham County, Georgia (d. 1967) [birth year disputed, may be 1894 or 1896]
Leela Majumdar, Indian Bengali writer known for her works for children, born in Calcutta, British India (d. 2007)
